name: Systems Thinking (Causal Loop Diagrams)
what_it_is: Understanding problems caused by feedback loops and system dynamics rather than linear cause-effect chains.
core_mechanic: Map key variables. Draw influence arrows. Label loops as reinforcing (R) or balancing (B). Identify the dominant loop. Find leverage points where small changes produce large effects.
when_to_use: Problem recurs despite apparent fixes (a balancing loop is resisting change) / An intervention made things worse / The problem feels "systemic" / Long-standing organizational problems
when_not_to_use: Acute one-time failures / Not enough system understanding to map variables / Quick diagnosis needed
ideaos_application: Agent 3 maps 3-5 most important variables, identifies the dominant loop type, locates the highest-leverage intervention point. Explains why previous attempts may have failed.
